Ad Text:
AEMO at the Heart of Energy  

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable, and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians. Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.

 

About the role  

Help shape the success of a newly established team within AEMO’s System Design division. In this dynamic, project‑focused analyst role, you will deliver high‑quality analytics, reporting, and business insights that drive strategic decision‑making and enhance project performance.

Reporting directly to the Group Manager, Business Solutions, you will play a pivotal role in standing up this new function within System Design. Your remit will include developing the division’s long‑term business strategy, creating digital, process and workforce planning roadmaps, embedding best‑practice risk and compliance frameworks, and leading continuous process improvement initiatives across the Division.

This is a unique opportunity to lead and influence ‘on‑the‑business’ initiatives by ensuring they are delivered on time, aligned to strategic objectives, and positioned to elevate the Division’s overall effectiveness and performance.

 

What you will be doing  

Develop, document, and implement the System Design Business Plan, embedding a regular performance monitoring and review cadence.

Conduct research and analysis to generate strategic insights that inform decision‑making and support long‑term planning.

Contribute to the System Design technology roadmap and collaborate with Digital teams to ensure effective interfaces, governance frameworks, and uplift of systems supporting forecasting, planning, onboarding, and connections activities.

Support project delivery and program oversight by analysing reports, maintaining dashboards, coordinating risk and compliance documentation, and ensuring operational information is accurate and up to date.

Drive operational improvements by coordinating initiatives, developing tools, templates and processes, and providing project management support to advance Division objectives.

 

What you will bring to the role  

Tertiary qualification in Engineering, Commerce, Business Administration, or a related discipline.

A wealth of experience in an analyst, strategy, or business support role, ideally with exposure to the energy sector.

Strong structured thinking and problem‑solving capability, with experience in business risk frameworks highly regarded.

Strategic, proactive mindset with the ability to distil complex or ambiguous information into clear, executive‑level insights.

Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across diverse teams and perspectives.

High attention to detail, strong prioritisation skills, and proficiency in Microsoft D365; experience with Power BI and Visio is an advantage.

 

In return some of our benefits to you  

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options. 

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more.

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year. 

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility). 

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks, and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP). 

 

About Our Process   

At AEMO we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome people of all backgrounds, including people with disabilities and neurodivergence and those who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. If you have any accessibility requirements to support you during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing us at talent@aemo.com.au

Ad Text:
Business Analyst | 12 Month Contract | Capital Markets

A leading consultancy is looking for an experienced Business Analyst to join a large-scale program within the capital markets space. This is a hands-on BA role suited to someone who is confident operating across both business and technical domains, with strong stakeholder engagement and workshop facilitation skills.

Key Responsibilities
* Elicit, analyse, and document business and system requirements
* Write high-quality user stories and acceptance criteria using Agile techniques
* Facilitate workshops with senior stakeholders and industry participants
* Manage stakeholder relationships across business and technical teams
* Define both functional and non-functional requirements
* Work across API integrations, messaging specifications, and data requirements
* Support the definition of testing requirements, including industry-facing test tools and data needs
* Contribute to process mapping where required

Key Requirements
* Strong Business Analyst experience across complex programs
* Proven experience in capital markets, this is essential
* Experience working with APIs, integrations, and data-focused initiatives
* Strong understanding of both functional and non-functional requirements
* Experience gathering and defining testing requirements
* Excellent stakeholder management and workshop facilitation skills
* Familiarity with Agile techniques, even if not working in a fully Agile environment

Highly Regarded
* Clearing and settlement experience
* BABOK certification

This is a great opportunity to join a high-profile program within a leading consultancy, working on complex, industry-impacting initiatives.

If you have strong capital markets experience and enjoy working at the intersection of business and technology, this role will be a strong fit.

This is a full time 6-month contract.

What we can do for you:  

At Specsavers, we know that to reach your full potential you need to work somewhere that will bring out your best. We pride ourselves on offering a workplace with a supportive, driven, flexible and inclusive culture. 

In 2025, we were proudly recognised as one of Australia’s Best Places to Work for the third consecutive year and ranked 12th on Fortune’s list of the World’s 25 Best Workplaces. 

Here’s a taste of just some of our perks: 

Quarterly bonus scheme 

Two free pairs of glasses each year 

On-site free parking 

Birthday, Volunteer, Professional Development and Paid Parental Leave 

Health & Wellbeing programs 

Fully funded social club – provides a wide range of activities throughout the year 

So, if you like the sound of working for a company that’s dedicated to improving the lives of both their people and customers - we think you’d look good at Specsavers.  

The role & team

Our dynamic and energetic Project Managers facilitate the delivery of technology solutions for a wide-ranging portfolio of exciting local and global projects. Operating within a matrixed Product Team, you will lead the delivery of data projects using agile methodologies, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives and technical requirements.

In your role as an IT Project Manager, you will be responsible for leading the delivery of data-focused initiatives across Specsavers ANZ, ensuring alignment with strategic goals and the broader data strategy. This includes managing scope, timelines and quality standards, while driving the effective execution of prioritised backlogs in line with business objectives and technical requirements


The skills we are looking for

Proven track record in leading data-centric IT projects through the full project lifecycle - from initiation to transition to support - including planning, stakeholder engagement, backlog prioritisation, and delivery monitoring (Ideally 5 - 10 years experience)

Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams including Data Engineers, Data Analysts, Business Analysts, Test Analysts and Reporting Analysts, ensuring alignment between business needs and technical execution.

Experience managing software development and data platform initiatives, including exposure to technologies such as Azure Data Factory, Databricks, and Power BI.

Deep knowledge of agile delivery methodologies, with hands-on experience in agile ceremonies, backlog refinement, and sprint planning for data initiatives.

Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build trust-based relationships and translate complex data concepts into business-friendly language.

Strong understanding of project management principles, methodologies & best practice and have worked in agile delivery methods

Formal project management qualifications are desirable (e.g., Prince2, Agile), especially where applied to data transformation or analytics initiatives.


About Us:

Specsavers are rated the market leader for eye tests, the retailer of choice for contact lenses and one of the fastest growing hearing providers in Australia and New Zealand. We are on a continual mission to transform eye and hearing health by eliminating preventable vision loss and blindness, including Glaucoma and Diabetes, through improved prevention and early detection, as well as providing accessible and affordable hearing care.

At Specsavers we believe a diverse, inclusive, and flexible culture enables our people to bring their whole selves to work and be proud of doing so. When it comes to flexibility, we understand that one size doesn't fit all. Our flexible working at Specsavers enables us to work in ways that support our lifestyle to help us balance our career with the other important things in life.

Creating a great place to work for our people is pinnacle to us and along with our Behaviours is at the core of everything we do. We like to think it’s a fun and positive place to work – why not have a look for yourself https://join.specsavers.com/au/department/support-office/

Applicants for the role are required to have current working rights as per, relevant government legislation.

Grow Your FP&A Career with Kraft Heinz!

As a lead finance business partner to the business and 2IC to the Head of FP&A, the FP&A and ZBB Reporting Manager provides critical finance support to internal and external stakeholders, including the ANJ executive team. With lead responsibilities over KHC business cycles and related reporting and submissions to the EPDM Zone, this position will be required to effectively interact with multiple levels of leadership across the organization. With a continuous improvement mindset, the FP&A and ZBB Reporting Manager will support the driving of the finance agenda cross the business.  




The Company

The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands.




Key position responsibilities include, but not limited to:

Responsibility of the monthly, quarterly, and annual business cycle (budget, forecast and performance reviews). 

Maintain and further develop KHC financial templates and decks that provides reliable information to the management team. 

Support the collation and presentation of monthly and quarterly business reviews to the Business Unit CEO/CFO along detailed variance explanations. Ensure that they are fully aware of the shape the business is in. 

Lead the development or improvement of existing processes relating to KHC business cycle/financial reporting. 

Manage business unit P&L and Cash flow (Australia, New Zealand, Japan, Korea): 

Develop metrics to track team-specific and company-wide performance. Identify opportunities, provide recommendations, and collaborate across all levels. 

Continuously challenge status quo and drive continuous improvement across all functions 

Lead Working Capital and Cash Flow Agenda for ANJ 

Oversee the management and control of fixed cost spend (ZBB), ensuring spend is according to policy and budgets while partnering with stakeholders to capture efficiencies and challenge performance. 

Responsible for ANJ reporting infrastructure, including:  

Optimizing our financial reports, providing accurate and timely visibility 

Ability to create and maintain conceptual and highly complex, efficient, logical financial models. 

Work with IT and FP&A Planning and Insights Manager to drive continuous improvements of KHC reporting software/systems 

Lead Data structure and availability to the business 

Manage and continue to develop the FP&A team 

Support other ad hoc requests/analysis/projects related but not limited to business profitability, long-range forecast and strategic plan 

Perform assigned tasks in accordance with all Kraft Heinz Food Safety, Environmental, Health and Safety, and Ethical and Human Resources policies, programs and goals. 




About you:

experience in FP&A, commercial finance or management accounting. 

Extensive people management experience 

Experience working on transformation projects, demonstrated competency in improving efficiency 

FMCG industry experience essential

Advanced Excel and financial modelling skills 

Advanced presentation skills, able to synthesise and communicate effectively with all levels of the business and cross functionally. 

Strong stakeholder engagement skills (including leading ELT forums), business partnering and communication skills. 

Commercial acumen and ability to interpret complex datasets and simplify messaging 

A drive to challenge the status quo, and demonstrate a continuous improvement mentality 

High attention to detail, analytical mindset, and ability to work under tight deadlines 

Demonstrated ability to work with and influence senior stakeholders




Benefits of working with us

Hybrid Working – Flexibility around when and where you work

Pay for Performance – industry leading variable compensation offerings tied to company and individual performance on top of a competitive base salary package

Parental Leave – paid time off for both primary AND secondary caregivers

Leave Options – Purchased leave, volunteer leave and LiveWell leave programs to give you even more flexibility to plan your life and spend time on causes that matter

Novated Leasing

Great Place to Work certification in Australia and New Zealand

In House Training Programs

Corporate Discount Programs 




Ready to make an Impact?

If you're passionate about S&OP, Continuous Improvement and project management we want to hear from you! This is a fantastic opportunity in a global FMCG organisation, so don’t miss out. We will be accepting applications on a rolling basis and reserve the right to close the job advertisement early.





 

Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes.

Your new company

You'll be joining a Canberra-based organisation operating within a security-led environment, with a strong focus on people, capability uplift and continuous improvement. The organisation is investing in the way it delivers People & Culture services, with a particular emphasis on improving employee experience, process efficiency and the quality of people-related data to support better decision-making. The culture values collaboration, thoughtful engagement and taking the time to understand the business before implementing change.


Your new role

As a Senior Business Analyst embedded within the People & Culture function, you will play a key role in analysing, mapping and improving people-related processes and employee experiences across the full employee lifecycle. Working closely with P&C leaders, internal stakeholders and technical partners, you will take a discovery-led approach to understanding business needs before shaping practical, people-centred solutions. The role also has a strong data focus, supporting the accuracy, integrity and integration of HR information to enable meaningful reporting and insights. This is a senior individual contributor role, suited to someone who enjoys influence, stakeholder engagement and delivery rather than people management.


What you'll need to succeed

Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst, ideally within HR, People & Culture, shared services or a similar environment
Strong capability in process mapping, analysis and redesign, with a focus on user or employee experience
A discovery first, consulting style approach with excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Experience working with data quality, reporting and system integration concepts, ideally across HR systems
The ability to work autonomously, manage competing priorities and operate effectively in a structured, security-conscious environment
Australian citizenship is required, along with the ability to obtain or hold a Baseline security clearance
Practical experience and approach are key

What you'll get in return

A permanent, full‑time role based in Canberra
The opportunity to work on meaningful, organisation wide people and culture initiatives
Exposure to senior stakeholders and the chance to influence how employee experiences are designed and delivered
A supportive team environment that values collaboration and thoughtful problem solving


What you need to do now

JOB OVERVIEW:

We are seeking an experienced Project Manager to lead the end-to-end delivery of a large-scale IBM Maximo redeployment, including BIRT reporting. This role is critical in ensuring seamless execution across planning, delivery, and transition phases while maintaining continuity of asset operations.

You will work across multiple teams and vendors, driving governance, managing risks, and ensuring successful integration, testing, and deployment in a complex enterprise environment; in the location of Melbourne (Adelaide optional) on Hybrid schedule.


Key Responsibilities:

Overall Delivery & Governance

Own end-to-end project delivery across planning, execution, cutover, and handover phases.
Establish and manage project governance, including cadence for steering, delivery forums, and status reporting.
Ensure alignment between business objectives, technical delivery, and operational readiness.

Planning & Wave Management

Develop and maintain the integrated project plan, incorporating the overlapping wave strategy.
Coordinate sequencing of activities to enable early readiness of integrations (e.g. BizTalk/AIS, GIS, Historian) and incremental testing ahead of final cutover.
Manage interdependencies across Maximo configuration, integrations, BIRT reporting, infrastructure, and data migration.

Stakeholder & Third-party Management

Act as the primary coordination point across Solution Architects, Infrastructure teams, delivery teams, and third-party providers.
Manage delivery constraints and risks arising from external or partially controlled environments.
Ensure clear ownership, decision making, and escalation paths are maintained throughout the engagement.

Scope, Schedule & Financial Control

Manage scope baselining and change control to prevent uncontrolled expansion.
Track milestones, deliverables, and critical path items.
Monitor project financials, including effort burn, forecast to complete, and variance reporting.

Risk, Issue & Dependency Management

Proactively identify, assess, and manage project risks and dependencies, with particular focus on integration readiness, data migration, and cutover.
Own issue and RAID management, ensuring timely escalation and resolution.
Ensure mitigation plans are in place for risks impacting operational continuity.

Quality & Delivery Assurance

Ensure quality gates are applied across design, build, test, and deployment phases.
Validate that deliverables meet agreed acceptance criteria and are fit for purpose.
Coordinate readiness reviews prior to key milestones such as integration testing, UAT, and go live.

Cutover, Handover & Transition

Lead cutover planning and execution, coordinating technical and business activities.
Ensure runbooks, support models, and operational KPIs are delivered prior to handover.
Manage transition to BAU support teams, ensuring a controlled and low risk go live.

Reporting & Communication

Provide clear, concise, and transparent status reporting to stakeholders.
Communicate progress, risks, decisions, and next steps effectively across technical and nontechnical audiences.
Maintain a single source of truth for delivery status and project artefacts.


Please apply with current resume in Microsoft Word format only (.doc or .docx). If you would like to have a confidential discussion, please contact Bidisha Swayamprava on Bidisha.Swayamprava@davidsonwp.com, quoting reference JN -032026-42801. Want to know more about Davidson? Visit us at www.davidsonwp.com

About the Company
This exciting technology business is headquartered in Australia, with international offices supporting customers in 120+ countries. It is focused on scalable growth, innovation, and building a digitally mature commercial function. The role is open to candidates based anywhere in Australia.

About the Role
This 12-month contract reports to the Head of FP&A and works across Finance, Sales, Product, and Marketing. You will lead the design and rollout of a global commercial operating model, partnering with external consultants and internal stakeholders to embed scalable systems, processes, and workflows.

Benefits

Opportunity to lead a global transformation program
Work with cross-functional international teams
Exposure to AI-enabled commercial innovation
High-impact role with strong executive visibility
Flexible, project-based contract environment
Duties / Key Responsibilities

Design and implement a global Quote-to-Cash framework
Lead the rollout of CRM and RevOps processes within HubSpot
Drive cross-functional alignment across commercial teams
Identify and implement automation and workflow improvements
Support AI-enabled process optimisation and documentation
Skills and Experience

Experience in RevOps, CRM optimisation, or business transformation
Strong process mapping and workflow redesign capability
Proven project management experience in cross-functional environments
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
High attention to detail with strong analytical capability

If you are interested in this position, please apply directly or contact Damien Sweeney at damien.sweeney@allurapartners.com.au.

The Company

At Tradeware we connect people with great products every day. We are a national business that services the Hardware industry with innovation, and we pride ourselves on being consumer-led and customer-driven.

We represent our own Brands and Brands of our supply partners and have long-term relationships with major hardware retailers. Tradeware offers local service and national strength through an experienced sales team, innovative marketing, and a unique network of nationwide distribution centres.

Our team are our heroes and are at the heart of everything we do. We look after our people and are passionate about continually expanding on our ‘People Experience’.

We believe that to achieve our business goals, every role in our business value chain is so important, and that we need to work together as one team. Together we will always aim high and strive to ensure we do the right things well.

The Opportunity

This role will partner with functional leaders across the business to improve commercial decision making and business performance through meaningful financial and non financial analysis, insights and recommendations.

Sitting across finance, data and operations you will help translate complex information into practical business actions. You will provide commercial and financial analysis, budgeting and forecasting support, business case analysis, dashboard and KPI development, and data driven insights that help leaders understand the commercial impact and value of their decisions.

You will work closely with stakeholders across the business and tap into the capability of our data team to drive stronger reporting, pressure test ideas, challenge assumptions constructively and identify opportunities to improve performance, processes and ways of working.

This role offers an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys business partnering, storytelling with data and influencing decision makers. You will play a key role in helping the business move from insight to action, while contributing to a culture of continuous improvement.

About You

You are likely already working in a Commercial Business Partner, Finance Business Partner, or similar partnering role, where you bring together strong financial capability, data-led thinking, and the ability to influence business decisions. You have a solid finance foundation and have grown your capability into the data and analytics space, using insights to support better performance, clearer decision-making, and continuous improvement.

To be the right candidate you will bring:

Demonstrated success in a similar commercial or finance business partnering role
A strong finance foundation, with experience extending into data and analytics
Experience influencing stakeholders and decision makers without direct authority
Strong capability in financial modelling, budgeting, forecasting and KPI development
Experience building or enhancing dashboards and translating data into meaningful business insights
The ability to simplify complex information and tell a clear story with data
A continuous improvement mindset, with experience identifying opportunities to improve performance, process or ways of working
Relevant tertiary qualifications in Finance, Accounting or a related discipline, with CA or CPA preferred
Experience with Power BI, and ideally exposure to SQL, Power Query and ETL/data warehouse concepts
Flexibility to travel interstate as required

Benefits:

Permanent full-time, secure role in a valued position
A strong friendly team based culture
Hybrid working arrangements with min 2 days a wweek in the office
National incentive scheme
Generous discounts on quality products
Learning & development opportunities
Employee assistance program (EAP) for you and your family
Free on site parking

This role is full time and based from our office in Dandenong South.

How to Apply

Applications will only be accepted through the seek website and confidentiality is assured.

To submit your application, please forward your resume and a brief cover letter outlining what value you can bring to our team?

Please note that whilst we will contact candidates with suitable experience and skills by phone, overwhelming response rates may mean that we are regrettably, unable to contact unsuccessful candidates personally.

No recruitment agencies, please

Sydney CBD | Hybrid working 4 days office, 1 day WFH

An ASX listed organisation with a complex, multi state operating footprint is seeking a Senior Group FP&A Analyst to join its high performing corporate finance team.


This role sits at the centre of group wide financial planning and analysis, partnering closely with senior leaders to deliver insight that directly informs strategic and commercial decision making. Based in the Sydney CBD, the position offers strong exposure to executive stakeholders, meaningful analytical work and clear long term career progression within a listed environment.


The Opportunity


This is a highly visible role suited to a commercially minded FP&A professional who enjoys working beyond routine reporting and wants to influence outcomes at a group level.
You will play a key role in shaping budgets, forecasts and business plans while contributing to strategic initiatives and continuous improvement across the organisation.


Key Responsibilities




Supporting the annual budget, business plan and rolling forecasts, including group consolidation and analysis
Delivering clear, insightful financial analysis to senior management to support decision making
Monitoring and analysing departmental costs across functions such as IT, Operations and Legal
Building, maintaining and enhancing financial models, templates and forecasting tools
Conducting scenario and sensitivity modelling to assess risks, opportunities and investment decisions
Providing ad hoc commercial analysis to support strategic initiatives and business growth
Tracking and analysing group KPIs and key operational drivers
Driving process improvement and best practice FP&A across the function


About You



CA or CPA qualified
Strong experience in budgeting, forecasting and financial planning within a large corporate or ASX listed environment
Demonstrated three way financial modelling experience is essential
Advanced Excel capability with strong PowerPoint skills
Commercial mindset with the ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights
Confident communicator with strong stakeholder engagement skills
Experience with business performance management tools such as IBM Planning Analytics is advantageous


Why Apply



Group level role within a large, complex ASX listed organisation
Exposure to senior leadership and strategic decision making
Clear pathways for career progression, mentorship and professional development
Competitive remuneration package
Hybrid working model
Strong focus on inclusion, wellbeing and long term employee development

JOB OVERVIEW:

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to support a large-scale enterprise system redeployment within the asset management and maintenance domain in the location of Melbourne (Adelaide optional) on Hybrid schedule. This role will play a critical part in ensuring business requirements, integrations, and reporting capabilities are accurately defined and delivered, enabling a seamless transition with minimal operational disruption.

Key Responsibilities:

Requirements & Process Alignment

Elicit and document business, operational, and reporting requirements across asset management and maintenance domains.
Map current state and future state processes, ensuring Maximo configuration aligns to the target operating model.

The Opportunity:
 
This is a rare find, a high-impact Senior Analyst role that puts you at the centre of executive decision-making, without the burnout that tends to come with it.
We're a small, listed company, small enough that your work is visible and will give you exposure to the CEO and the ELT, and large enough that the problems are genuinely complex and consequential. There's no bureaucratic filter between your analysis and the people acting on it.
The role exists to serve as a strategic and analytical force multiplier for our GM Supply Chain, extending their reach across strategy, commercial, procurement and operational topics, and ensuring that every significant decision is grounded in rigorous, clear-eyed insight.
 
What You'll Actually Do:
 
You'll be the analytical engine behind some of our most important decisions. That means synthesising messy, ambiguous inputs into crisp executive conclusions; building business cases and scenario models that hold up under scrutiny; assessing markets and opportunities with the same rigour you'd apply in consulting but with the satisfaction of seeing your work directly shape outcomes.
 
Specifically, you'll:

Act as a trusted analytical partner to the GM Supply Chain and senior leaders across operations strategy, commercials, and procurement.
Prepare and quality-assure executive papers, owning the narrative, the assumptions, and the decision-readiness.
Conduct market and opportunity assessments, including TAM/SAM/SOM, competitive dynamics and strategic fit.
Analyse operational economics, including contribution, margin, breakeven, total cost of ownership, and translate them into clear investment and capability decisions.
Lead and support strategic procurement initiatives.
Model business outcomes across scenarios and provide the upside/downside perspectives executives need to make risk-adjusted calls.
Keep transformation initiatives honest, tracking milestones, benefits and risks, and providing independent challenge where things drift.
Who We're Looking For:
 
You've ideally come from a Big 4 or top-tier consultancy, or from a corporate finance, strategy or in-house analytics function where the work was demanding and the standards were high.
 
You don't need to tick every box. What matters most is that you're:

Highly analytical and commercially sharp so you can move fluidly between big-picture strategy and granular financial modelling.
A structured thinker, you can take an ambiguous problem, frame it clearly, and turn it into something decision-ready.
A strong communicator; your written and verbal output is clean, confident and calibrated for executive audiences.
Comfortable challenging up so you'll push back when assumptions don't stack up, and you'll do it with evidence.
A hands-on executor, you simplify complexity without losing the substance, and you follow through.
Formally, we'd expect a business-related degree, around seven or more years of relevant experience, and demonstrated capability across strategy, commercial modelling and financial analysis. Exposure to procurement or operational transformation is a plus.
 
Why This Role:

Direct access working closely with the GM Supply Chain and have genuine visibility to the CEO and broader ELT. Your work won't disappear into a slide deck; it will drive decisions.
Scope without the grind, the problems are meaty and varied, but this is a senior individual contributor role with no people or P&L responsibility. You own your work, not a team.
Flexibility - we know that the right person for this role may be weighing lifestyle considerations. We're open to conversations about how the role is structured, and we'll trade on flexibility for the right fit.
Scale that lets you matter; as a listed company of our size, there's no hiding behind layers of hierarchy. What you produce has a direct line to business outcomes.
If you're a partial match but the shape of this role appeals, we'd still encourage a conversation.

You will collaborate closely with product managers, data analysts, engineers, and go-to-market stakeholders to balance high-quality user experiences with diverse business and product objectives. Working within cross-functional squads, you will create innovative solutions through your expertise in user research, interaction design, visual design, and user validation methods while solving design problems at scale across multiple Asia-Pacific markets.

The team operates with a strong agile mindset, embracing rapid experimentation and frequent feature releases, offering a start-up feel backed by SEEK's scale and support. You will own and help drive best-practice experiences across SEEK's insights ecosystem, advocate for users in business decisions, and influence design priorities for continuous uplift while ensuring products continue to set industry standards for clarity, usability, and decision support.

What you'll do

Plan and scope design initiatives aligned with product outcomes and timelines, balancing progress over perfection in experimental contexts.
Shape product direction and roadmap by understanding customer needs, platform capabilities, and industry trends in recruitment and user experience.
Lead end-to-end design efforts including research, workshopping, prototyping, usability testing, and data analysis for complex challenges.
Collaborate with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to solve problems, balance trade-offs, and deliver impactful solutions.
Communicate and defend design decisions through compelling storytelling, ensuring alignment with company objectives and user insights.
Leverage and evolve the Design System for consistency, creating bespoke components and interaction patterns when necessary.
Design inclusive solutions tailored to the cultural and linguistic diversity of Asia-Pacific audiences.
Investigate emerging technologies including AI and personalization to enhance platform functionality and user experience with advanced AI tool usage.
Qualifications

Essential skills and experience 

5+ years of experience in online or software product design, ideally at marketplace or SaaS company with significant senior-level experience.
Strong user research capabilities with proficiency in planning, conducting research, usability testing, and generating actionable insight reports.
Ability to define design plans balancing high-quality outputs within fast timeframes, exercising strong judgment on when to go deep versus lean.
Ability to context-switch effectively across concurrent tasks and rapidly process complex design, research, and stakeholder requirements and input.
Proven ability to transform customer insights into beautiful, effortless experiences with expertise in polished UI and accessibility standards.
Skilled in leading design thinking techniques, especially workshop facilitation for scope guidance and fast ideation in agile environments.
Advanced proficiency in Figma with strong verbal and written communication skills to articulate design decisions aligned with business goals.
Advanced usage of AI tools across design process stages to accelerate outputs, insights, and deliverables with comfort in fast-paced iteration.

Desirable skills and experience

Familiarity with research and analytics tools like HotJar, Intercom, and Dovetail or similar.
Knowledge of collaboration tools such as Jira, Confluence, and Miro.
Ability to align design decisions with both user and business goals.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, iterative environment with shifting priorities.
Desire to work in a start-up like environment, open to try and learn things in a fast-paced way of working.

Ad Text:
At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues.​ We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high-performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.

Your New Role

Reporting to the Corporate Communications and Reporting Manager, the Corporate Reporting Lead is responsible for leading the Fund's approach to corporate reporting and the development, implementation and management of relevant corporate narratives and communication strategies in a way that enhances the Fund's trustworthiness and transparency, and positions AustralianSuper as Australia's leading super fund in the world's best system for members.

Key duties include but are not limited to:

Manage the preparation and delivery of AustralianSuper's suite of corporate reports (e.g. Annual Report, financial reports, Member Outcome Assessments), ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and best practice benchmarks.
Work collaboratively with colleagues across the Fund and coordinate data collection and validation from internal stakeholders to support accurate reporting.
Maintain reporting schedules, expense trackers, and registers ensuring public information is current and accessible.
Agree and document content management responsibility among relevant teams in relation to the About Us and other corporate sections of the website and ensure the website governance registers are current.
Engage with Fund colleagues and senior management to support corporate reporting and communication deliverables and provide guidance and support as required.
Nurture and maintain key internal and external relationships, including contractors as required.

What You'll Need

Demonstrating strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to adapt to tailor messaging to diverse topics, situations and stakeholder needs.
Applying both strategic and tactical approaches to communications, supporting organisational objectives and stakeholder needs.
Crafting clear, compelling long-form content and building strategic messaging that enhances an organisation's reputation.
Managing multiple stakeholders across all levels across an organisation to deliver excellent results.
Organising and prioritising work to achieve business outcomes, often within fast-paced and complex environments.
Managing complex projects, including the ability to prioritise tasks, manage workflow and adapt to changing circumstances.
Showing a genuine interest in corporate affairs, issues management and a members first approach.

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results.

Melbourne | Hybrid Working | Enterprise Environment

We are partnering with a leading Australian organisation to appoint a Lead Architect – Data, a pivotal role within a high-performing enterprise architecture and innovation function.

This is a rare opportunity to step into a strategic, high-impact position where you will shape the future of data across a complex, large-scale environment.

The Opportunity

As the Lead Architect – Data, you will be responsible for defining and driving the enterprise data architecture strategy, ensuring platforms and capabilities are scalable, secure, and aligned to long-term business goals.

You will work closely with senior stakeholders across business and technology to translate strategy into practical, actionable roadmaps and solutions.

This role sits at the intersection of strategy, architecture, and innovation, offering genuine influence across enterprise-wide initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the end-to-end data architecture strategy across operational and analytical platforms

Develop and maintain target state architectures, roadmaps, and design patterns

Partner with stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable data solutions

Drive alignment across data, integration, analytics, and governance capabilities

Contribute to architecture governance, standards, and best practices

Identify and evaluate emerging technologies and innovation opportunities

Support strategic planning, including investment prioritisation and capability uplift

Build strong relationships across business and technology to ensure successful delivery outcomes

Mentor and uplift capability across the architecture and broader delivery teams

About You

You bring:

Proven experience in enterprise or solution architecture within complex environments

Highly Regarded

Why This Role?

Work in a role with true enterprise-wide influence

Ad Text:
About Central Steel Build


In 1975, Central Steel Build began as a small family business with a humble, customer focused vision: to purpose build high quality sheds which would stand the test of time. We're now well into our second generation, and while our design and fabrication methods have become more sophisticated with cutting-edge modelling technology added to the mix, we've stayed true to our ideology of continuous improvement.

Our values shape us

Creating client value – we have a 'customer first' approach, driven by innovation, speed and attention to detail.

Working together – we demonstrate team energy by emulating a trusting, collaborative and results-focused workplace.

Continuous improvement – we see change as a smart opportunity to remain nimble, adaptable and accountable.

Being respectful – we foster a positive and humble mindset that listens and encourages.

Openly communicating – we have honest and fun two-way communication that is constructive and responsive.

Fast-Growing Manufacturing Business

Central Steel Build is embarking on a major AI, automation, and systems transformation. With a turnover of ~$65M and strong growth ambitions, we are implementing a full company-wide AI and automation roadmap.

 We are now searching for a strategic and hands-on technology leader who can own the architecture, build the data foundations, and lead our evolution into a modern, data-driven manufacturing organisation.

Reporting to the CEO, this role has enterprise‑wide accountability for architecture, data platforms, system integration, analytics, and AI governance.

The Role

The Head of Technology & Data is a strategic and hands‑on leader responsible for:

Designing and owning the enterprise architecture.

Establishing CSB’s AI operating model and governance.

Leading all system integration, automation, and data strategy.

Building and maintaining the Unified Analytics Layer.

Ensuring CSB has clean, reliable, real-time data to power AI agents, automated workflows, reporting, and future innovation.

Acting as the key executive partner to our external AI consultants while embedding capability inside CSB.

This is a role for a CTO-level leader who can think strategically AND tactically execute. 

This is a lead-by-doing and lead-by-thinking role. While you are not expected to build all solutions personally, you must be deeply comfortable with the detail – able to engage credibly with infrastructure architects, software engineers, data specialists, and vendors, and make informed decisions that balance technical excellence with business value.

About You

You will thrive in this role if you have:

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field.

8+ years of experience across technology leadership, enterprise architecture, or data engineering

Experience integrating or architecting around NetSuite or a similar ERP

Strong hands-on capability in Microsoft ecosystems, SQL, Azure, ETL/ELT pipelines, and Power BI.

Understanding of APIs, integrations, and workflow automation

A passion for agentic AI, automation, and improving how people work.

Experience in manufacturing, construction, fabrication, or supply chain environments (preferred)

You enjoy both long-term strategy and rolling up your sleeves to build.

 You exhibit professional traits, including:

Exhibiting excellent judgment and high standards

Thinking strategically, but staying on top of tactical execution

Thinking big and standing firm in your convictions

Being results-oriented

Developing great people and being able to inspire passion in others

 Why Join Us?

Exciting opportunity: drive transformation for a growing business delivering superior results.

Great team culture: Join a positive, collaborative work environment that is strongly values-driven.

State-of-the-art facilities: Work in a modern, cutting-edge building alongside a skilled, multidisciplinary team.

Lead data, integration and automation at the centre of Banyule's transformation journey.

Banyule City Council is undertaking a significant transformation to become a more connected, insight-led and efficient organisation, better equipped to meet growing community expectations, increasing service complexity and the need to deliver more value from every dollar. As Data, Integration and Automation Coordinator, you will play a key leadership role in that journey.

This is more than a technical role. It is an opportunity to help shape how Council works into the future by strengthening our data foundations, improving how our systems connect, and unlocking smarter, simpler ways of working across the organisation. You will lead the development of scalable data, integration and automation capability that supports better decision-making, reduces fragmentation and manual effort, and enables more responsive, modern services for our community.

Working across multiple business areas, you will help move Banyule towards a more joined-up and digitally enabled operating environment. Your work will contribute directly to Council's broader transformation agenda by improving information flow, building organisational capability, and embedding solutions that support both immediate service uplift and long-term strategic change.

Why you'll love this role and what you'll do:

As Data, Integration and Automation Coordinator, you will help turn Banyule's transformation ambitions into practical, organisation-wide outcomes. You will work at the intersection of strategy, delivery and enablement, partnering across the business to uplift capability, improve systems and processes, and help Council operate in a more connected and contemporary way.

You will:

Lead Council's data and insight capability by improving the quality, accessibility and governance of data, supporting stronger evidence-based decision-making, service planning and organisational performance.
Drive integration and information flow across Council by connecting systems, reducing duplication and fragmentation, and improving how information moves between platforms, teams and business processes.
Deliver automation and digital efficiency initiatives by identifying opportunities to streamline repetitive work, improve consistency, reduce manual effort and strengthen compliance through smarter use of technology.
Play a key role in organisational transformation by helping embed new ways of working, lifting digital and data maturity, and translating technical capability into meaningful business and community outcomes.

This is a leadership role for someone who can combine strategic thinking with practical delivery. It requires a person who can see the bigger picture, build strong relationships across the organisation, and bring others along through change. You will be part of shaping a more modern Council, one that is better connected internally and better able to serve its community externally.

This role is part of Banyule's Leadership Network; you'll have opportunities to grow your skills and experience of leadership in a Local Government context and develop your skills as an organisational leader beyond your role-based expertise.

What you'll bring/what we are looking for:

We're looking for a strategic, forward-thinking and collaborative professional who can see the bigger picture and turn data, systems and technology into practical organisational improvement. This role will suit someone who enjoys influencing at a strategic level, leading meaningful change, and working across the organisation to strengthen data capability, improve connected systems and drive smarter, more efficient ways of working. You'll bring a strong blend of technical expertise, sound judgement and stakeholder leadership, with the ability to translate complexity into clear direction, practical solutions and measurable outcomes. You'll also bring:

1. A degree in Data Science, Information Systems, Computer Science, Business or a related field, or significant equivalent experience.

2. Demonstrated experience developing and implementing enterprise data strategies, governance frameworks and data policies.

3. Extensive experience delivering data, system integration and automation solutions at scale.

4. Proven ability to lead strategic projects and deliver measurable organisational outcomes.

5. Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to influence, build consensus and work effectively across all levels of the organisation.

6. Advanced understanding of data compliance, privacy and cybersecurity standards.

7. Personal values and behaviours aligned with Banyule City Council's values, with a commitment to collaboration, psychological safety and service excellence.

Pre-employment requirements:

Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Check (NCCHC)
Psychometric Assessment

What's on offer

A permanent, full-time role
Salary range $142,754.50 to $153,950.90 per annum (excluding super)
26 weeks of paid parental leave - primary carer/4 weeks of paid parental leave - partner  
Ongoing learning and development opportunities, including health and well-being initiatives
Flexible work and leave arrangements, hybrid work arrangements

Ready to make an impact?

Apply now and shape the way Banyule connects data, systems and technology to deliver smarter services.

How to apply:

Please submit your resume and a cover letter (maximum of three pages) setting out your achievements, skills and experience aligned to the key selection criteria in the position description. Please also respond to the job-specific questions in the application form.

For more information about this position, please view the position description. For a confidential discussion, we encourage you to contact Tim Wakeham on 03 9242 3450.
Applications close: 3 April 2026 at 11:45 PM.

Why choose this opportunity?At Banyule City Council, we value innovation, collaboration, and professional growth. Banyule City Council is an Equal Opportunity Employer; we value diversity and inclusion, and we welcome candidates from all backgrounds. If you have a reasonable adjustment, support, or access requirement, we encourage you to inform us through your application or email employment@banyule.vic.gov.au

Our Values: Our employees align their careers with Banyule because they share our values of respect, integrity, responsibility, initiative, and inclusion. They thrive in our strong learning and development culture and the positive way we work in partnership with the community. You will share our values and have a passion for helping a community of over 130,000 residents to live happier, healthier and more connected lives, all while growing your skills and advancing your career. We look forward to you starting the next chapter of your career at Banyule Council.

Diversity Statement: Our community is made up of diverse cultures, beliefs, abilities, bodies, sexualities, ages, and genders. We are committed to access, equity, participation, and rights for everyone: principles that empower, foster harmony, and increase the well-being of an inclusive community.

To discover more about Banyule's commitment to advancing gender equality in the workplace, please find Banyule's Workplace Gender Equality Action Plan 2021-2025.

Acknowledgment of the Traditional Custodians: Banyule City Council is proud to acknowledge the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as Traditional Custodians of the land, and we pay respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elders, past, present and emerging, who have resided in the area and have been an integral part of the region's history.

Banyule City Council endorses the Uluru Statement from the Heart in full and accepts the invitation to walk with First Nations peoples, to a better future for us all.

Child Safe Standards Statement of Commitment: Banyule City Council is a child-safe organisation committed to the safety and well-being of children.

Council has a zero tolerance for child abuse. All allegations and safety concerns will be treated seriously and acted upon. As a child-safe organisation, we are committed to providing a child-safe environment where children feel safe, are empowered, valued and protected. Council will actively listen to children, ensuring their voices are heard and considered in decisions that affect their lives.

Company description:



About us
The Department of Government Services (DGS) was established on 1 January 2023 to improve everyone's experience of doing business and interacting with the Victorian government. We bring important day-to-day services together in one department to make things easy and seamless for Victorians and businesses. We are doing this by connecting and digitising our systems and platforms across state and local government as well as streamlining our corporate and procurement services.



Job description:



The Digital Platforms Security team requires a security specialist with experience in customer facing application development and operations. Preferably including mobile application development and delivery. The successful candidate will have experience in security threat analysis, vulnerability management, incident response, secure application development practices. Participation in governance and audit activities will also be required.

The ideal analyst will work alongside the security team to develop and provide guidance to all areas of the organisation for the protection of user information and minimisation of risk. The successful candidate will be able to demonstrate the ability to communicate and work outside their team to both educate and facilitate improvement on security matters.

The ideal analyst will be accustomed to taking complex systems, problems and scenarios and conducting the relevant research and applying their own problem-solving skills, experience and personal judgement to generate actionable outcomes.

This position is to support the departments uplift and maturation of data storage and the security of that data and will be tasked with critical project support tasks involving detailed analysis of ISM (Information Security Manual) requirements, development of a gap analysis and plan to implement PROTECTED controls within the department.

This position is only open to applicants with relevant rights to work in Australia.

For specific responsibilities please see the Position Description.

How to apply:
Apply before the advertised closing date 11:59pm on Sunday, 5 April 2026. All applications should include:

a resume; and
a cover letter which addresses the key selection criteria (within three pages)

Other relevant information

The department is committed to providing and maintaining a working environment which is safe and without risk to the health of its employees and clients and consistent with the department's obligations under the Occupational Health and Safety Act 2004 (OH&S Act), to support this DGS has a COVID-19 Vaccination policy which strongly recommends all DGS employees be fully vaccinated.

Preferred candidates will be required to complete a Pre-employment Declaration and Consent Form. Employment of successful candidates will be subject to a National Police Check.

DGS actively promotes diversity, inclusion and an equal opportunity workplace. We welcome applicants from all diverse backgrounds, including people with disabilities and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. All roles at DGS can be worked flexibly, however it may differ from role to role.

Job Title: Product Owner, Corporate Technology (Workday & Enterprise Systems)
Ad Text:
Challenger Limited is an ASX-listed investment management firm managing $128.2 billion in assets (as at 31 December 2025). Life with us is fast moving and always exciting. Together we’re driving to deliver our vision to provide our customers with financial security for a better retirement.

We achieve this goal by providing a work environment where people from diverse backgrounds, with a range of skills and experiences can contribute and succeed.

Product Owner, Corporate Technology (Workday & Enterprise Systems)

Make meaningful impact - your prioritisation and delivery decisions will improve the tools used by Finance, HR, Risk, Legal and Company Secretariat.

Own the roadmap end-to-end across the corporate systems landscape (Workday at the core)—set priorities, make trade-offs on investment, and steer outcomes.

Lead through influence - partner with internal teams and Accenture to balance run and change and deliver controlled releases that improve the platform.

We offer a flexible hybrid model based out of our Martin Place, Sydney CBD office - typically three days in-office and two days from home.

About the opportunity

You’ll sit at the centre of our corporate applications, owning the product direction for platforms that are business-critical and highly visible. With Workday at the core, you’ll shape what we deliver next across the broader corporate systems landscape - turning complex needs into clear priorities and outcomes.

You’ll work across Workday plus a broader suite of corporate platforms (including Anaplan, Workiva and Archer), partnering with subject-matter experts to land integrated, end-to-end outcomes.

You won’t be doing it alone: you’ll partner with internal teams and our delivery partner Accenture to move from discovery to delivery. Success in this role looks like a platform landscape that stays stable and resilient, while still evolving - through a steady cadence of well-managed change.

Your focus will be on three core areas:

Hands-on ownership of the corporate systems backlog and roadmap (Workday at the core) from intake and triage through to prioritisation and release.

Balancing run and change: platform health, defect/incident prioritisation, upgrades and controlled releases

Partnering with vendors to deliver outcomes to agreed scope, timelines and service expectations (including SLAs)

Product Ownership

Own product outcomes end-to-end across Workday and adjacent corporate systems—from discovery through to delivery and adoption—turning ambiguity into clear options and measurable improvements (not just outputs).

Bring Workday and enterprise systems understanding to spot end-to-end impacts early (including integrations, reporting, and non-functional needs like reliability and performance).

Partner with Accenture and internal teams to sequence work, manage dependencies and keep stakeholders aligned through simple, consistent governance and planning cadences.

Shape the platform direction over time - driving upgrades, automation and continuous improvement opportunities that reduce friction and risk for the business.

Agile Delivery & Vendor Partnership

Keep delivery moving - set clear priorities and run the key agile cadences with your partner-led squad so work is ready to release.

Hold vendors and partners to account (including SLAs), using regular service reviews and clear escalation to lift quality, reliability and delivery outcomes.

Bring a risk-aware mindset - embed controls, compliance and security into change so releases are auditable and well-governed.

What we’re looking for

We’re looking for a Product Owner who enjoys shaping priorities, making trade-offs, and delivering outcomes through others. You’ll be comfortable leading through influence across internal teams, senior stakeholders and delivery partners, and you’ll bring demonstrated agile delivery experience - planning sprints/releases, managing dependencies and driving outcomes with cross-functional teams.

You’ll do well in this role if you:

Are a strong problem solver - able to diagnose issues, run option analysis, and unblock delivery in partnership with business and vendors.

Are comfortable working across an enterprise systems landscape (multiple platforms, integrations, vendors and business areas).

Are confident owning a roadmap and backlog and aligning stakeholders to a shared set of priorities.

You’ll also bring:

Solid Workday experience, with sound knowledge of configuration, integrations, and core process flows.

Experience delivering through a partnered model (internal teams + Accenture), including vendor/SLA management and budget/spend awareness.

Effective workshop facilitation and requirements discovery skills, turning business needs into clear user stories and acceptance criteria.

Experience with corporate Finance/HR systems and end-to-end business processes (including downstream reporting impacts).

Confidence running a live platform—triaging incidents/defects, prioritising fixes, and coordinating end-to-end change across integrations and testing.

Why Challenger?

At Challenger, we’re small enough to be agile, but big enough to accelerate bold ideas. We support your growth and development, offering flexibility and a culture that values your unique contributions. 

Competitive discretionary bonus scheme 

18 weeks paid parental leave for all new parents 

Challenger Day – one extra day off every year in recognition of the effort our people make.  

Additional support leave (fertility, gender affirmation) 

Extra superannuation contributions 

Employee share plan 

Employee Assistance Programme 

Subsidised on-site café and central location near Martin Place Metro 

Access to free onsite yoga, mindfulness and Pilates classes. 

Fitness Passport

Access to annual free flu shots & skin checks

https://www.challenger.com.au/about-us/careers/our-benefits 

#LI-KM1 

#LI-Challenger 

#LI-Hybrid

Challenger's employee value proposition guides how we work: Grow and realise your potential, supporting each other, stronger together and making things happen. Our culture encourages curiosity, considered thinking and meaningful contribution, with opportunities to build a broad and rewarding career.

We are committed to fostering a safe, inclusive and respectful workplace where people of all backgrounds, identities and ways of thinking can thrive, and promoting flexible working to support work-life balance. 

Challenger is proud to be a Workplace Gender Equality Agency (WGEA) Employer of Choice for Gender Equality, a Family Friendly Workplace and recognised as a Bronze Employer in the Australian Workplace Equality Index (AWEI), the national benchmark for LGBTQ+ workplace inclusion.

Job Title: Product Owner, Corporate Technology (Workday & Enterprise Systems)
Ad Text:
Challenger Limited is an ASX-listed investment management firm managing $128.2 billion in assets (as at 31 December 2025). Life with us is fast moving and always exciting. Together we’re driving to deliver our vision to provide our customers with financial security for a better retirement.

We achieve this goal by providing a work environment where people from diverse backgrounds, with a range of skills and experiences can contribute and succeed.

Product Owner, Corporate Technology (Workday & Enterprise Systems)

Make meaningful impact - your prioritisation and delivery decisions will improve the tools used by Finance, HR, Risk, Legal and Company Secretariat.

Own the roadmap end-to-end across the corporate systems landscape (Workday at the core)—set priorities, make trade-offs on investment, and steer outcomes.

Lead through influence - partner with internal teams and Accenture to balance run and change and deliver controlled releases that improve the platform.

We offer a flexible hybrid model based out of our Martin Place, Sydney CBD office - typically three days in-office and two days from home.

About the opportunity

You’ll sit at the centre of our corporate applications, owning the product direction for platforms that are business-critical and highly visible. With Workday at the core, you’ll shape what we deliver next across the broader corporate systems landscape - turning complex needs into clear priorities and outcomes.

You’ll work across Workday plus a broader suite of corporate platforms (including Anaplan, Workiva and Archer), partnering with subject-matter experts to land integrated, end-to-end outcomes.

You won’t be doing it alone: you’ll partner with internal teams and our delivery partner Accenture to move from discovery to delivery. Success in this role looks like a platform landscape that stays stable and resilient, while still evolving - through a steady cadence of well-managed change.

Your focus will be on three core areas:

Hands-on ownership of the corporate systems backlog and roadmap (Workday at the core) from intake and triage through to prioritisation and release.

Balancing run and change: platform health, defect/incident prioritisation, upgrades and controlled releases

Partnering with vendors to deliver outcomes to agreed scope, timelines and service expectations (including SLAs)

Product Ownership

Own product outcomes end-to-end across Workday and adjacent corporate systems—from discovery through to delivery and adoption—turning ambiguity into clear options and measurable improvements (not just outputs).

Bring Workday and enterprise systems understanding to spot end-to-end impacts early (including integrations, reporting, and non-functional needs like reliability and performance).

Partner with Accenture and internal teams to sequence work, manage dependencies and keep stakeholders aligned through simple, consistent governance and planning cadences.

Shape the platform direction over time - driving upgrades, automation and continuous improvement opportunities that reduce friction and risk for the business.

Agile Delivery & Vendor Partnership

Keep delivery moving - set clear priorities and run the key agile cadences with your partner-led squad so work is ready to release.

Hold vendors and partners to account (including SLAs), using regular service reviews and clear escalation to lift quality, reliability and delivery outcomes.

Bring a risk-aware mindset - embed controls, compliance and security into change so releases are auditable and well-governed.

What we’re looking for

We’re looking for a Product Owner who enjoys shaping priorities, making trade-offs, and delivering outcomes through others. You’ll be comfortable leading through influence across internal teams, senior stakeholders and delivery partners, and you’ll bring demonstrated agile delivery experience - planning sprints/releases, managing dependencies and driving outcomes with cross-functional teams.

You’ll do well in this role if you:

Are a strong problem solver - able to diagnose issues, run option analysis, and unblock delivery in partnership with business and vendors.

Are comfortable working across an enterprise systems landscape (multiple platforms, integrations, vendors and business areas).

Are confident owning a roadmap and backlog and aligning stakeholders to a shared set of priorities.

You’ll also bring:

Solid Workday experience, with sound knowledge of configuration, integrations, and core process flows.

Experience delivering through a partnered model (internal teams + Accenture), including vendor/SLA management and budget/spend awareness.

Effective workshop facilitation and requirements discovery skills, turning business needs into clear user stories and acceptance criteria.

Experience with corporate Finance/HR systems and end-to-end business processes (including downstream reporting impacts).

Confidence running a live platform—triaging incidents/defects, prioritising fixes, and coordinating end-to-end change across integrations and testing.

Why Challenger?

At Challenger, we’re small enough to be agile, but big enough to accelerate bold ideas. We support your growth and development, offering flexibility and a culture that values your unique contributions. 

Competitive discretionary bonus scheme 

18 weeks paid parental leave for all new parents 

Challenger Day – one extra day off every year in recognition of the effort our people make.  

Additional support leave (fertility, gender affirmation) 

Extra superannuation contributions 

Employee share plan 

Employee Assistance Programme 

Subsidised on-site café and central location near Martin Place Metro 

Access to free onsite yoga, mindfulness and Pilates classes. 

Fitness Passport

Access to annual free flu shots & skin checks

Senior Equities Analyst – System Capital (Melbourne)

An opportunity has arisen within System Capital for a Senior Equities Analyst to join our Melbourne-based investment team.

System Capital is a bottom‑up, absolute‑return equities investment manager with a research philosophy centred on understanding the full dynamics of a business ecosystem.

Founded in 2022 by Lev Margolin, former Portfolio Manager at L1 Capital, System Capital brings together over 20 years of investment experience and a strong track record of delivering risk‑adjusted returns.

We are looking for a Senior Equities Analyst who will work closely with the Portfolio Manager, lead high‑conviction research, shape investment debates, and contribute meaningfully to the ongoing build-out of a growing investment business.

What you’ll do

Lead deep fundamental research, including industry mapping, company due diligence, financial modelling, and behavioural/structural analysis.
Generate independent investment insights, form differentiated views, and present actionable recommendations to the Portfolio Manager.
Influence portfolio construction by contributing to stock selection, position sizing discussions, and risk assessment.
Enhance and refine research processes, including the development of analytical tools, data workflows, or frameworks that elevate the team’s capability.
Engage directly with company management teams, industry experts, and external research providers.

What we’re looking for

5–15+ years’ experience in equities research or financial analysis (buy‑side, sell‑side, private equity, M&A, or strategy/consulting).
Advanced analytical capabilities and expert-level financial modelling skills.
A demonstrated and sustained passion for equity markets, business analysis, and long‑term investing.
Strong academic foundation in finance, economics, or related STEM fields.
Highly self‑directed, curious, and comfortable working in a high‑accountability environment.
Someone who thrives in a small, entrepreneurial, high‑performance investment team and is committed to excellence.

If you’re ready to step into a senior analytical role with meaningful influence and long-term career upside, we’d love to hear from you. Please apply directly to the role for further discussion.

Job Title: Financial Planning and Analysis Reporting Specialist
Ad Text:
$145K–$155K base salary plus super, with a hybrid work model

Brand-new FP&A role with exposure to global operations, senior stakeholders and system implementations

Strong development focus, inclusive culture and long-term career pathways

At Dyno Nobel Limited our people vision is to unlock our people capability and culture to create safe, inclusive and high performing teams on the ground.

As the FP&A Reporting Specialist, you will support the DNEL Business Unit by delivering high-quality financial reporting, analysis and planning insights that enable informed decision-making and strong financial governance across a diverse, global portfolio.

Working closely with finance teams in multiple regions (across Europe, Middle East, Africa and Latin America), you will play a key role in month-end reporting, budgeting and forecasting cycles, business performance analysis and continuous improvement initiatives, including system and process enhancements.

·         Partnering with regional finance teams to ensure strong governance, compliance and data integrity

·         Strong financial accounting and management reporting capability within a complex or multinational environment

Assist the business teams to implement system change into their ways of working and transitioning to industry best practice.

Business Analyst – Finance & Procurement (SAP S/4HANA)

Emanate Technology is partnering with a large, complex organisation undergoing a major ERP transformation to source an experienced Business Analyst with strong finance and procurement domain expertise.

This role offers the opportunity to play a key part in a significant SAP S/4HANA implementation, working closely with business and technical stakeholders to drive process improvement, enable change and deliver tangible outcomes.

About the role

As the Business Analyst, you will support a large-scale transformation across finance and procurement functions, with a strong focus on SAP S/4HANA. You will work across current-state and future-state processes, identify gaps, document impacts, and translate business needs into clear, actionable solutions.

This is a hands-on role requiring deep engagement with stakeholders, contribution to testing and training activities, and close collaboration with technical and change teams to ensure successful adoption of new systems and processes.

What you will be doing
Analyse and evaluate AS-IS and TO-BE business processes across finance and procurement domains
Identify, document and communicate gaps between current and future states
Elicit, analyse and document complex business requirements and translate them into process maps and functional artefacts
Work closely with business owners, SMEs and technical teams to align requirements and solutions
Contribute to UAT planning, test script development and execution
Identify risks and provide practical, data-driven recommendations
Support training and readiness activities to ensure successful system adoption
Partner with change resources to articulate future-state processes and clearly define business impacts
Build strong, trusted relationships across multiple stakeholder groups in a complex operating environment
What you will bring
6+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst, ideally across finance operations, procurement and accounting
Strong experience working with ERP platforms, including SAP S/4HANA and/or SAP ECC 6.0 (FICO, procurement-related modules)
Proven capability in end-to-end business process mapping and analysis
Solid experience supporting testing (UAT), training preparation and documentation
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts into business language
Strong analytical mindset and ability to operate effectively in complex, fast-moving environments
High attention to detail, governance awareness and a structured approach to delivery
Bonus points for…
Experience working within Queensland Government or other large public sector environments
Exposure to tools such as SAP Signavio or similar process modelling platforms
Business Analyst certification (e.g. CBAP)
Experience working across finance and procurement functions simultaneously
