Seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join our Design team. 

$190,026 base salary plus superannuation

Permanent FT | Haymarket. Grow your career with us today.  

  

About Ausgrid – we’re wired for good 

We’re the largest distributer of electricity on Australia’s east coast, which means over two million homes and businesses rely on our good work. We’re here at the heart of the energy transition, and we’re driven by a powerful purpose to make electricity accessible for all. 

It’s an exciting new chapter for our company with each day bringing a renewed energy to how we deliver, store and use electricity. As we continue to grow, we’re in search of exceptional talent to help us achieve our goals.  

Everyone’s job is important here. Our customers come from all walks of life, and so do we. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, respectful, and inclusive workplace, and encourage applications from all backgrounds. 

  

See yourself working here 

At Ausgrid, we’re building intelligent, data driven solutions that help power communities across NSW. We’re looking for a Senior Data Scientist to design, develop and deploy AI and advanced analytics solutions that directly support our business objectives and integrate seamlessly into our enterprise data landscape. You will be based at our Haymarket office and reporting to Manager of Data Services and Platforms.  

This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys solving complex problems, working end to end across the AI lifecycle, and partnering closely with the business to deliver real outcomes. 

You’ll collaborate with stakeholders, testers and SMEs, translating business use cases into robust AI solutions that create measurable value. 

This a unique role to work on Work on meaningful AI solutions that support critical infrastructure and communities and work for a business that is at the heart of the energy transition. 

 

Key responsibilities 

AI & Analytics Development: Design, build, train and deploy ML and LLM based models to address specific business use cases, integrating with existing systems and platforms. 

MLOps & Deployment: Leverage Ausgrid’s data platform and established MLOps/LLMOps practices to operationalise models at scale. 

Performance & Monitoring: Implement monitoring, alerting and cost management routines to assess model performance and reliability. 

Data Modelling & Serving: Build Lakehouse aligned data models (Bronze/Silver/Gold), optimising for performance, reliability and cost. 

Security & Governance: Apply strong data security controls, encryption, secrets management and audit logging, in line with governance and regulatory requirements. 

CI/CD & IaC: Contribute to CI/CD pipelines, version control and environment promotion using Azure DevOps/Git and infrastructureascode (Bicep). 

Operations & Support: Monitor production jobs and SLAs, perform root cause analysis and work with support teams to resolve issues. 

Collaboration & Documentation: Engage with stakeholders throughout delivery and produce clear technical documentation, runbooks and data dictionaries. 

 

What you’ll bring  

Our hiring decisions are not only based on your experience and skills, but your passion, attitude, and alignment to our core values.  

 

Essential experience and skills 

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or a related discipline. 

Strong software development experience using Python, Spark and SQL. 

Proven experience working with Azure and largescale, cloud based data workflows. 

Solid database design and optimisation experience (e.g. PostgreSQL). 

Working knowledge of machine learning workflows in Databricks. 

Experience with automated unit and integration testing. 

Familiarity with Agile delivery and MLOps practices. 

Strong full stack mindset with the ability to learn quickly and deliver scalable, high quality solutions. 

  

Life at Ausgrid  

At Ausgrid, we go beyond just a competitive salary to offer an exciting range of benefits. From salary sacrificing electric vehicles and novated leasing, to discounted corporate health insurance and access to Fitness Passport, our benefits support your work here, and your life out there.  

Celebrate success every day with our employee recognition program to bring out each other’s best. Looking to grow professionally? We’ve got you covered with study assistance, secondments, training and mentors, whose advice you’ll keep wherever you go.  We’re proud to offer gender-neutral parental leave to balance work when growing your family. And if you’re caring for elderly parents, our aged care support services offer expert guidance to help you access care for your loved ones. At Ausgrid, it's all about supporting you in every aspect of life. 

  

If you require support with your application/reasonable adjustments for the interview process, please reach out to our dedicated Talent Team recruitment@ausgrid.com.au.

The SunRice Group is an Australian ASX-listed branded food group, comprising multiple businesses, assets and operations across Australia, New Zealand, the Middle East, the United States, The Pacific Islands and Asia.  SunRice was built by the Australian rice industry over many decades and today is one of Australia’s leading branded food exporters, with approximately 2,000 employees and operations in 11 countries.

SunRice is a great place to work, providing a real opportunity for passionate people to make a difference.

 

About the role

As our Supply Chain Finance Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in ensuring we continue delivering high‑quality, sustainable products while driving value across our ANZ operations.

Sitting at the heart of our supply chain, you’ll provide end‑to‑end finance support across Procurement, Costing and Inventory - enabling smarter purchasing decisions, clear and reliable costing, and continuous improvement across the business. You’ll work closely with Operations Finance, Commercial & Category Finance, and Procurement, turning data into insight and insight into meaningful impact.

You’ll thrive in a fast‑paced environment where resilience, curiosity and problem‑solving are essential. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with large datasets, building models in Excel or Power BI, and influencing decisions across a wide network of stakeholders.

 

 

Key responsibilities will include:

Deliver month‑end reporting across ANZ procurement, co‑manufactured products and grain commodities, including PPV and inventory performance.

Lead forecasting and budgeting cycles for inventory and procurement function

Support costing roll‑ups, standard costing and NPD financial assessments

Own core data areas including standard costs, inventory data integrity, SLOB management

Identify and deliver project savings and optimisation initiatives within the supply chain

Partner with procurement and operational leaders, providing insights that drive commercial decision‑making

Contribute to continuous improvement across systems, processes, and reporting

 

About You

You’re a natural problem‑solver who is curious, collaborative and thrives in a fast‑moving environment. You know how to work with large datasets, untangle complexity, and present insights with clarity and purpose. You enjoy working with people as much as numbers, and you bring resilience and resourcefulness to every challenge.

 

You will bring:

2–4 years’ experience in management accounting, finance or business analysis

Background in supply chain, FMCG or procurement‑related finance (advantageous)

Strong Excel capability and confidence working with large datasets; Power BI experience highly valued

Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify and influence

Experience partnering with teams such as Procurement, Operations Finance, Commercial or Category Finance

SAP experience, ideally in costing modules (preferred)

A degree in Finance, Accounting, Commerce, Economics or Information Systems

 

What’s in it for you

At SunRice Group, we believe great work starts with feeling supported, valued and inspired. When you join our team, you’ll enjoy a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, growth and everyday life.




SunRice Product Allowance: Take home a taste of what we do best. You’ll receive a weekly allowance of SunRice Group products, so you can enjoy and share the food we’re proud to make.

Leave: Enjoy competitive leave benefits, including volunteer leave and the option to purchase additional leave when you need extra flexibility.

Wellness Reimbursement: Your wellbeing matters. Twice a year, you’ll be reimbursed for fitness memberships or other health and wellbeing initiatives that help you feel your best

 

If you enjoy getting into the detail, simplifying complexity, and supporting teams that keep food moving from farms to families, this is a place where your work truly matters.

 

All correspondence will be dealt with in strict confidence.

 

SunRice is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. SunRice will consider qualified applicants for employment based on their merit, individual skills, qualifications and other appropriate criteria relevant to the role for which they are applying.

At Wesfarmers Health we strive to make health, beauty and wellness experiences simpler, more affordable and easier to access for all Australians.

Our portfolio includes well-known names like Priceline, as well as our MediAesthetics brands - Clear Skincare Clinics and SILK Laser Clinics. In the digital space, we're proud to have SISU Health and InstantScripts—and most recently, we've launched our newest retail beauty stores, atomica.





About us:

What began in 1910 as a small co-operative of just three pharmacies has grown into Wesfarmers Health—a leading Australian organisation in health, beauty, and wellness.

Today, Wesfarmers Health proudly employs over 3,600 team members, all committed to delivering accessible, innovative, and trusted health and beauty services to Australians nationwide, across retail, medi-aesthetics, digital health, and wholesale and supply chain operations.

What you will do:

We are looking for a Strategy & Transformation Business Partner to join the Wesfarmers Health team. Reporting to the Senior Strategy Manager, you will play a crucial role in delivering strategic projects and shaping the future direction of our business.

This role combines analytical rigour with commercial thinking and stakeholder engagement. You will work across the full breadth of strategy and transformation, partnering with stakeholders at all levels to solve complex problems and translate strategy into execution.

In this role, you will:

Lead the identification and resolution of key opportunities through structured analysis, synthesis and iteration
Manage smaller independent projects and deliver multiple workstreams within larger strategic initiatives
Develop actionable plans alongside business unit owners to translate strategy into execution
Monitor market trends to identify competitive threats and new business opportunities
Partner with the business development team to evaluate attractive markets and potential targets
Contribute to the annual Corporate Planning process, including industry analysis and business plans for strategic initiatives
Support initiative owners with analysis, business case development and strategy execution

What you will bring:

Minimum of 2-3 years experience in investment banking, strategy consulting or corporate development
A Track record of providing high-quality commercial advice to senior management
The ability to apply critical thinking across both strategy and value creation initiatives
Experience drawing insights from multiple data sources to identify key opportunities
High learning orientation and the agility to work with stakeholders across a broad range of topics

Why us?

Access to employee benefits across Wesfarmers including team member discounts at Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks, OnePass, Priceline, atomica and Clear Skincare Clinics
Ongoing professional development and career opportunities across the Wesfarmers Health Division and the broader Wesfarmers Group
Novated leasing options

Ad Text:

Brisbane CBD

The Opportunity

We're looking for an experienced Change Manager to lead end-to-end change across a diverse, multi-stakeholder environment including students, academic staff, and professional teams.

You'll play a key role in driving adoption of a new AI-enabled platform, ensuring users are ready, confident, and engaged from launch through to long-term sustainment.

Key Responsibilities
Change Strategy & Planning

Develop and deliver a comprehensive change management plan aligned to phased delivery

Conduct stakeholder analysis and impact assessments across diverse user groups

Identify risks, resistance points, and mitigation strategies early

Define and track success metrics for adoption and behavioural change

Stakeholder Engagement & Communications

Deliver clear, targeted communications that build trust and set expectations

Translate complex AI concepts into plain-language messaging for varied audiences

Facilitate workshops, engagement sessions, and feedback loops

Establish and support a network of change champions across faculties and business units

Training & Readiness

Develop user guides, FAQs, and training materials

Support content owners in understanding governance and ongoing responsibilities

Align change readiness with technical delivery milestones

Adoption & Reinforcement

Monitor post-launch adoption and user confidence

Deliver reinforcement strategies to embed new behaviours

Capture feedback and feed insights into product and delivery teams

Support transition to business-as-usual ownership

Key Requirements

Proven experience leading change on technology projects end-to-end

Strong understanding of AI adoption challenges

If this is something you'd like to be considered for click APPLY NOW or contact David at david.reynolds@talentinternational.com

At Smartways, we specialise in time-critical, complex logistics for the Australasian healthcare community. Our mission is to lift patient outcomes and save lives across all healthcare industries – including medical devices, life sciences, pharmaceuticals, and clinical trials.

As a tech-driven organisation, 95% of our orders are processed online or through seamless system integrations. We leverage AI-optimised routing, real-time GPS tracking, live ETAs, and automated proof-of-delivery – making technology core to every delivery we make.

The Role
The Head of Analytics, AI & Operations is a strategic, senior role responsible for transforming data into intelligence that drives operational excellence across Smartways’ 24/7 healthcare logistics network.

This role goes beyond traditional reporting: the Head of Analytics will harness the power of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and advanced analytics to optimise critical services delivery, improve driver and network efficiency, strengthen customer reporting, and support evidence-based decision-making at every level of the business.

Working at the intersection of data science, AI, and healthcare logistics operations, this leader will build scalable analytics capabilities that directly enhance patient outcomes, reduce operational risk, and support Smartways’ ambitious growth across Australia and New Zealand.

Key Responsibilities:

Analytics & Reporting

Collect, analyse, interpret, and present data from across the business to enable high-quality operational and executive decision-making

Use Power BI, Metabase, and emerging AI-native reporting tools to create visually immersive, interactive, and real-time dashboards for Operations, Customer Service, Finance, and Executive teams

Prepare management reports that clearly articulate trends, patterns, and forward-looking predictions using relevant operational data

Tie together disparate and unrelated data sets – including driver GPS data, POD records, booking system data, temperature logs, and financial data – to produce unified, actionable insights

Perform continuous quality assurance on all data pipelines and reporting, detecting breaks, anomalies, and integrity issues and determining solutions

AI & Advanced Analytics

Design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning models to optimise operations – including predictive routing for time-critical deliveries such as Patient on Table (POT), Direct Drive, and Next Flight services

Build and refine AI-generated routing and dispatch logic that accounts for live traffic conditions, driver availability, and urgency classification to reduce delivery times and improve service reliability

Develop predictive analytics models for demand forecasting, fleet utilisation, and driver capacity planning across all states and New Zealand

Leverage AI to identify operational risk patterns – including late deliveries, temperature excursions, and chain-of-custody gaps – and surface early-warning insights to operations leadership

Evaluate and implement AI tools, large language models (LLMs), and automation capabilities that enhance workflow efficiency across operations, customer service, and dispatch teams

Champion the use of generative AI for internal productivity – including prompt engineering frameworks, AI-assisted report narration, and intelligent alerting systems

Operational Strategy & Stakeholder Engagement

Work alongside Operations, Customer Service, Finance, and IT teams to establish current business intelligence needs and prioritise the analytics roadmap accordingly

Lead cross-state workforce planning initiatives, influencing and governing cross-functional leaders and teams through data-driven insights rather than direct authority

Translate complex analytical findings into clear, practical recommendations that allow operational stakeholders to take immediate, realistic action

Facilitate collaborative planning sessions with stakeholders, aligning subject matter expertise with organisational strategy and data capability

Build trust and credibility with cross-functional stakeholders by embedding data and AI tools into everyday decision-making workflows

Drive accountability and execution by guiding teams from insight to action, ensuring all analytics initiatives are understood, owned, and delivered 

Data Infrastructure & Governance

Make recommendations on data collection methods, system integrations, and analytics infrastructure to improve data quality, coverage, and analytical efficiency

Collaborate with IT to ensure data from Smart Systems (Track & Trace, live GPS, POD, mobile booking, label generation) is structured and accessible for analytics and AI workloads

Maintain data governance standards consistent with ISO 27001 certification requirements and healthcare-sector data security obligations as required

Academic Qualifications

Tertiary qualifications, preferably in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, or a related analytical discipline, with a high level of academic achievement

Postgraduate study, certifications, or specialised training in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or Applied Data Science is highly regarded

Professional certifications in AI/ML platforms (e.g. Microsoft Azure AI, Google Cloud AI, AWS Machine Learning) or data visualisation tools are advantageous

Ad Text:
Company description:



Manager, Innovation, Strategy & Delivery (VPS6) | 12‑month backfill

Fixed-term, Full-time | $138,631 - $185,297 + super | 121 Exhibition St, Melbourne + hybrid

Too often, good ideas stall in governance. Promising pilots never scale. Innovation becomes a slide deck rather than a delivery discipline. This role ensures that innovation is governed well, delivered well, and scaled where it matters.

About VIDA

The Victorian Infrastructure Delivery Authority (VIDA) is a specialist project delivery organisation, responsible for delivering the biggest transport and health infrastructure program in Victorian history. This includes more than 200 projects such as the Metro Tunnel, North East Link, West Gate Tunnel, removing level crossings, new and upgraded hospitals in Footscray, Frankston and Warrnambool, and specialist programs such as the early parenting centres expansion and upgrade program.

The unprecedented program of works brings together expert skillsets to deliver crucial health and major transport projects and ensure that operations are streamlined and efficient while delivering better outcomes for Victorians. Led by the Director-General, VIDA is comprised of the four project offices Major Road Projects Victoria, Level Crossing Removal Project, the Metro Tunnel Project, and the Victorian Health Building Authority.

Within VIDA Services, The Hive is VIDA's improvement and innovation capability — helping teams solve complex problems, build innovation capability, pilot practical solutions (including emerging tech), and scale what works across programs for measurable impact.



Job description:



About this Opportunity

The Manager, Innovation, Strategy and Delivery is a senior strategic leadership role providing enterprise-level oversight and delivery of innovation programs across VIDA. You'll contribute to (and at times lead) strategic planning, enable capability uplift, design supportive frameworks and governance, and lead cross‑agency engagement that helps embed a culture of innovation.

You will:

Design, implement and continuously improve enterprise innovation programs and capability initiatives.
Strengthen innovation governance and alignment with whole‑of‑government and VIDA objectives.
Provide high-level strategic advice on innovation systems, transformation approaches and change enablement; manage strategic risks within public sector governance/probity/financial frameworks.
Lead complex, high‑visibility initiatives across multiple stakeholders and dependencies.
Lead and support a small team of innovation and technical advisors; produce executive‑level briefings and communications.

About You

You're a senior operator: strategic, credible, and delivery‑minded. Able to work across boundaries and turn ambiguity into forward motion.

You'll bring:

Experience leading innovation or transformation in complex, multi‑stakeholder environments.
Proven ability to build frameworks/systems that uplift capability and deliver measurable value.
Demonstrated, hands‑on experience using AI and emerging technologies in complex organisational environments.
Strong analytical and advisory skills with confidence influencing senior leaders and partners.
Leadership experience building high‑performing, collaborative teams.

Key Selection Criteria

Demonstrated experience in leading innovation or transformation initiatives in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
Proven ability to develop and implement strategic frameworks, systems, or initiatives that enable innovation capability uplift and value delivery.
Advanced conceptual and analytical skills, with a track record of providing authoritative advice to senior leaders and informing strategic decision-making.
Proven ability to manage high-value relationships and influence senior leaders, delivery partners, and external stakeholders.
Demonstrated leadership experience, including managing high-performing teams and fostering a collaborative, values-driven culture

What can we offer you?

An emphasis on work / life balance
High-performing team environment
Equal opportunity employer
Personal & professional development opportunities
Committed to safety & wellbeing
Training & development opportunities

Pre-Employment Checks

The successful candidate will be required to undergo pre-employment checks which include national police/qualification (where applicable) checks and misconduct screening.

How to Apply

Please include a CV and a cover letter (maximum 2 pages) addressing the Key Selection Criteria. To help us assess your application, please use short headings aligned to the criteria and include concise examples outlining what you did, how you did it, and the outcome (where relevant). Please click here to view the position description.

For a confidential discussion, please contact Reza Mohammed, Hive Innovation Lead at reza.mohammed@vida.vic.gov.au.

Applications close 11:59 pm - Monday 6 April 2026.

Job Title: Senior Finance Business Partner - Commercial Excellence
Ad Text:
Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Commercial Performance and Governance team as a Senior Finance Business Partner – Commercial Excellence!

 

Stay and grow with us!

14% superannuation (for EA team member) is standard here - because your future matters.
We’re committed to your growth, offering top-tier training and development to help you build a career you’re proud of.
Enjoy the flexibility of hybrid work, balancing time at our purpose-built hubs with working from home.
When you're in the office, take advantage of our thoughtfully designed spaces: end-of-trip facilities, yoga and multi-faith rooms, parent and carer rooms, quiet zones, BrewHub coffee stations, and collaborative team areas.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As the Senior Finance Business Partner – Commercial Excellence, you will play a pivotal role in driving the organisation’s commercial management uplift across the enterprise, collaborating closely with key stakeholders to enhance Performance and Partnering Operations. You will work with internal and external stakeholders to deliver the Commercial Excellence program through shaping business initiatives, conducting in depth analysis and providing recommendations, to drive value. 


 

Day to day, you'll:

Work alongside the Commercial Performance and Partnering community to standardise and automate business processes, standardise data, ultimately providing the building blocks for advanced analytics and automation.
Delve into financial data, identifying key trends and insights to inform decision-making, and support the provision of recommendations that drive cost excellence at ART.
Manage the governance of the factbook that financial benefits and baselines will be derived from at ART, so that benefits can be tracked and realised in a well-documented and governed manner.
Lead the design and the development of management insight related allocation tools (IT Cost Allocations), supporting the ongoing cost discipline of ART.
Proactively identify and address reporting challenges, anticipating potential issues and implementing solutions to ensure smooth and accurate reporting in an automated manner.


This role is available as a 12-month opportunity initially.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Professional accounting qualifications such as Institute of Chartered Accountants (CA ANZ), Certified Practising Accounts Australia (CPA) or equivalent.
Proven experience in a role that provides critical and actionable expenditure insights into financial performance.
Strong understanding of cost allocation processes and behaviours such processes drive.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely, and influence at senior/executive management level.
Proven experience in the preparation and presentation of financial and non-financial reports for senior/executive stakeholders.
Experience working on cost efficiency programs.
Advanced level Excel and Word skills.

Python coding ability is desirable. 

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Friday, 3 April 2026.

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

A bit about us:

At Interactive, our customers trust us with their most important data, critical devices and security. We’re Australian owned, 500+ strong, and built for high-performance managed services.

Our Cloud services span 24/7 managed services, migrations and consulting across multiple public clouds plus our own private cloud.

The role:

As our Head of Cloud Procurement, you’ll own the commercial engine behind our Cloud portfolio - governance, vendor strategy, pricing inputs and cost optimisation. You’ll relentlessly hunt cloud cost-reduction opportunities, sharpen our data and processes, and partner closely with Sales, Presales and Product to land deals, protect margin and drive sustainable growth.

Some of the key things you'll do:

Be the commercial connector across presales, delivery, finance and procurement - aligning outcomes end to end.
Own and optimise the cost base for cloud services - driving efficiency without compromising delivery.
Lift Cloud budgeting, forecasting and margin management - building cadence, accountability and visibility.
Chair the Pricing Review Board - set guardrails, test assumptions, and ensure pricing is commercially right.
Lead deal reviews with commercial insight, risk appetite and sharp financial modelling.

What we’re looking for:

Proven experience procuring Cloud and aligned services (ideally in an MSP or cloud-native environment).
Strong knowledge of the IT vendor and services landscape - commercial models, levers and negotiation angles.
Deep capability in cost-stack analysis across complex cloud environments.
Strong financial acumen - able to translate complex cost structures into clear commercial decisions and outcomes.
Executive-level stakeholder management - credible, persuasive and comfortable driving change.

Ready to make Cloud procurement a competitive advantage? Apply now, we’d love to meet you.

Join Corrs Support Services, Australia's leading independent law firm.

We’re seeking an experienced Business Analyst Manager to lead, mentor and elevate our Business Analysis capability across the firm. This is a senior, dual‑purpose role that combines people leadership with hands‑on delivery—bridging business strategy and technical execution.

Reporting to the Head of Portfolio Delivery, you’ll ensure that all analytical outputs are robust, value‑driven and implementation‑ready, enabling Project Managers, QA and delivery teams to execute with confidence. You’ll also play a critical role in shaping standards, improving quality, and embedding consistent ways of working across a complex portfolio.

This is an ideal role for a seasoned BA leader who enjoys influence, coaching others, and staying close to complex, high‑impact work.

What you’ll be doing

Professional leadership & people management

Lead and line‑manage a team of Business Analysts, including recruitment, onboarding and performance management
Build capability across the BA team, strengthening analytical, technical and stakeholder engagement skills
Establish and enforce quality standards, including a clear “Definition of Ready” for all BA artefacts
Standardise and maintain BA tools, templates and approaches to ensure consistency across the firm
Partner with portfolio leadership to allocate BA resources based on capability and business priorities

High‑impact project delivery

Act as a lead practitioner on the firm’s most complex initiatives, setting the benchmark for best practice
Ensure strong alignment between Business Analysts and Project Managers, preventing siloed delivery
Facilitate collaborative requirement validation sessions to ensure requirements are understood, validated and value‑driven before build
Review and quality‑assure BA outputs to ensure they are technically and operationally ready for delivery and testing

Testing & quality governance (approx. 20%)

Act as the final quality gate for the portfolio, ensuring solutions do not progress without signed‑off testing outcomes
Define and maintain testing and quality standards across manual and automated testing approaches
Provide direct leadership and guidance to the QA Tester, ensuring alignment between requirements and test coverage
Support UAT planning and execution that balances business availability with rigorous validation
Lead defect triage for complex issues and provide Go / No‑Go recommendations based on risk
Essential criteria

To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Extensive experience in Business Analysis and project delivery, with 3–5+ years in a leadership or management role
Proven people leadership capability, including mentoring, performance management and capability uplift
Strong experience delivering complex initiatives, bridging business needs and technical execution
Deep understanding of business analysis frameworks, such as IIBA / BABOK
Confidence working across Waterfall, Agile and Hybrid environments, applying the right approach based on risk
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to influence at all levels
Strong analytical, problem‑solving and documentation skills, with high attention to detail
Comfort working with project and delivery tools, such as Smartsheet or similar platforms

About us:

Corrs Chambers Westgarth is Australia’s leading independent law firm. We provide exceptional legal services across the full spectrum of matters, including major transactions, projects and significant disputes, offering strategic advice on our clients’ most challenging issues.

Corrs’ culture is characterised by our values of excellence, commitment, collaboration and respect. Our policies, practices and behaviours foster a supportive and inclusive workplace, and we are focused on creating opportunities for our people and supporting a diverse workforce.

Flexible working as standard – we have established flexibility as standard business practice and an option for all our people.
Bonus leave – we provide an additional week of leave each year (5 weeks annual leave in total)
Parental leave – we offer 26 weeks of gender-neutral paid parental leave and pay superannuation on both paid and unpaid leave (up to 12 months)
Wellbeing program – we support the wellbeing of our people across areas including resilience, mindfulness, personal development, physical health and self-care.
Diversity and Inclusion – we set meaningful goals and take action to improve diversity and foster an inclusive culture in which all employees feel supported and valued.
Responsible business approach – we have an active pro bono and community engagement program and we are a member of the United Nations Global Compact. We are also a member of AusLSA, which works to promote and increase sustainable practices across the legal sector.
Learning and development opportunities – including our Australian-first international study scholarship, a national training program and mentoring initiative.

To apply click on the "apply" button or contact Shannon Garrett at shannon.garrett@corrs.com.au for a confidential discussion.

For more information, see our website

Not just through the infrastructure we operate, but through the way we show up for each other, our customers’ and our communities. The future of energy is in our hands. Let’s shape it together.




We all want a future of energy we can be proud of. With us you can.




We are looking for an experienced Strategy Lead to help shape AusNet’s future direction by delivering high‑quality insights and strategic recommendations, influencing senior leaders’ decision‑making, and supporting enterprise‑wide strategic planning and communications.




As part of Finance & Strategy, this role contributes to driving strategic alignment, with a focus on long-term performanceacross the organisation.




What you’ll be doing in this role:

Leading strategic deep dives and developing insights that guide Board and Executive decision‑making.

Solving complex, ambiguous problems independently across business units.

Supporting long‑term strategic agenda development for the business.

Simplifying complex technical topics into compelling, executive‑ready narratives.

Develop strong working relationships across business units and levels.




You don’t need to check every box, however we’re looking for a good combination of:

Consulting experience gained in a top‑tier or boutique consulting environment, with 2 to 3+ years working on complex, ambiguous, business‑critical problems.

Strong stakeholder engagement skills – confident working across all business units, extracting insights from SMEs, influencing senior leaders, and presenting to Board‑level audiences.

We are seeking a NetSuite Administration Manager to take ownership of a critical ERP platform. You'll manage day-to-day operations, drive system enhancements, support users, and collaborate with external partners to ensure seamless integrations and maintain data integrity. This is a great opportunity for an experienced ERP professional to make a real impact in a collaborative and dynamic environment.

The Role:
In this role, you will:

Manage daily NetSuite ERP operations, ensuring system stability, performance, and reliability

Configure and maintain roles, permissions, workflows, dashboards, and sandbox environments

Provide end-user support, training, and documentation to maximise adoption and efficiency

Coordinate system enhancements, integrations, and manage vendor/partner relationships

Identify opportunities for process improvement and support continuous system optimisation

Maintain data governance, master data integrity, and operational controls

Assist with project delivery, testing, and change management initiatives

Skills & Experience:

Minimum 5 years' experience supporting NetSuite or similar ERP systems

Strong technical understanding of ERP administration, integrations, and system support

Experience managing external vendors and implementation partners

Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and problem-solving skills

Relevant qualifications in Information Systems, Business, Finance, or a related discipline

NetSuite certification or equivalent ERP expertise highly desirable

Why Apply:

Take full ownership of a critical ERP platform

Work in a collaborative and supportive environment

Lead ERP system improvements and integrations

Opportunity to have a tangible impact on system efficiency and user adoption

How to Apply:
If you are an experienced NetSuite professional looking for a hands-on role with significant responsibility, apply now with your resume and cover letter.

The Role

As a NetSuite Solution Consultant, you will be the trusted advisor for customers during delivery. You'll lead workshops, define solution architecture, and work closely with project managers and technical teams to ensure successful execution.

Success in this role looks like clear decision-making, strong stakeholder alignment, and high-quality delivery outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Lead customer discovery and solution design, translating business needs into end-to-end NetSuite solutions

Facilitate solution workshops, driving clear outcomes and aligned decisions

Create and maintain solution design documentation for both clients and delivery teams

Act as the functional lead across implementation projects, ensuring delivery stays on track

Collaborate with technical consultants and third parties to deliver integrated solutions

Recommend process improvements and leading practices aligned to business goals

Support testing, validation, and go-live readiness

Contribute to scoping, estimations, and statements of work

Identify and manage risks and dependencies throughout delivery

Provide mentorship to consultants and support pre-sales activities where required

About You

You're a senior NetSuite professional who can confidently lead delivery, influence stakeholders, and make sound solution decisions in dynamic environments.

Key requirements:

6-8+ years' NetSuite consulting experience, including end-to-end implementations

Strong experience designing integrated business solutions across systems and teams

Solid understanding of core finance processes (Record-to-Report, Procure-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash)

Proven ability to engage and influence senior stakeholders

Strong problem-solving skills with a practical, delivery-focused mindset

Experience managing multiple concurrent projects

Strong knowledge of the NetSuite platform (configuration, roles, permissions, reporting, core modules)

Ability to balance best practice vs real-world business needs

Bachelor's degree in Business, IT, or related discipline (preferred)

Why Apply

Work on complex, high-impact transformation projects

Join a collaborative, high-performing delivery team

Competitive salary aligned to experience and impact

Flexible hybrid working arrangements

Opportunity to grow into senior leadership roles

Exposure to well-known organisations and strategic projects

If you're passionate about leading delivery, solving complex problems, and building strong client relationships, we'd love to hear from you.

Senior Finance Process Architect – P2P

Location: Melbourne (hybrid)
Day Rate: $1000 – $1100 per day + GST
Contract Duration: Initial 3 months + 12-month extension
Engagement: Part-time (2–3 days/week) - potential 5 days per week at extension

The Role
We’re looking for a hands-on Finance Process Architect to review and improve core finance processes, with an initial focus on Procure-to-Pay (P2P).
You’ll assess current processes, identify inefficiencies and control gaps, and design future-state processes to support a major ERP transformation.

What You’ll Do

Review and map end-to-end finance processes (P2P focus)
Identify risks, inefficiencies, and manual workarounds
Design and document improved processes (SOPs, RACIs, workflows)
Support implementation, testing, and training
Work closely with finance stakeholders to drive adoption
What You Bring

Strong experience across finance processes (P2P, ideally broader)
ERP experience (SAP preferred)
Process mapping / improvement expertise (Lean, BPMN etc.)
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Experience in finance transformation or ERP readiness

ERP upgrade / transformation experience (e.g. S/4HANA)

Job Title: EOI Finance Professionals at Trilogy Care
Ad Text:
Trilogy Care is one of Australia's fastest-growing health and aged care providers, and we're building the finance function to match. Not a traditional back-office. A modern financial operations team where accountants write SQL alongside journal entries, where Python replaces copy-paste, and where AI agents handle the work that used to eat your week.

We're opening expressions of interest from finance professionals who have the technical rigour of a CPA/CA but think like systems builders. If you've ever looked at a month-end close and thought "I could automate this" - we want to talk to you.

Who we're looking for

We're connecting with finance and accounting professionals at all levels - junior, mid-level, and senior accountants, financial analysts and emerging finance leaders ready for their next step.

Across management accounting, financial reporting, commercial finance, and operations - we have roles available now with more to come as the team scales.

What makes you a fit

You don't wait for a manual. You write it in Claude Code and then automate it.

Open to AI: You have an open mind to learn and apply the latest in AI thinking. You're not waiting for permission — you're already experimenting with LLMs, copilots, and automation in your day-to-day work, and you want an employer that encourages it.
